STT 1, 008 + [Enuma Eliš 4][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[... la si-ma]-⸢ti⸣-[šu]

[...] inappropriately his,

r 2'2'

[...] ir-tuš ⸢it⸣-[muḫ]

[...] he attached (it) on his chest.

r 3'3'

[...] i-⸢sa⸣-[du]

[...] he smote [...],

r 4'4'

[... u₂-ša₂-pu]-⸢u₂⸣ šu-ri-[šam]

he had silenced [...] ... [...],

r 5'5'

[... AN].ŠAR₂ ⸢UGU⸣ [...] ⸢ka⸣-liš uš-zi-[iz-za]

he had established totally Anšar's [...] over [...],

r 6'6'

[... dNU].DIM₂.MUD ⸢ik⸣-[šu-du] ⸢dAMAR.UTU⸣ qar-[du]

Marduk the hero had achieved [...] of Nudimmud,

r 7'7'

[... DINGIR]-⸢MEŠ⸣ ka-mu-⸢ti⸣ ṣi-⸢bit-ta⸣-šu u₂-dan-nin-[ma]

he strengthened his captivity [...] bound [gods],

r 8'8'

[...] ⸢ta⸣-a₃-wa-⸢ti⸣ ša₂ ik-mu-u₂ i-tu-ra ar-[kiš]

he turned back [...] Tiamat whom he had defeated.

r 9'9'

[ik-bu]-us-ma be-⸢lu₄ ša₂ ta-a₃⸣-wa-ti i-šid-[sa]

The lord trampled upon Tiamat's base,

r 10'10'

[i-na mi]-⸢ṭi⸣-šu la pa-di-i ⸢u₂⸣-lat-ti muḫ-[ḫa]

[with] his merciless mīṭu-weapon he shattered the skull.

r 11'11'

[u₂-par]-ri-iʾ-⸢ma⸣ uš-lat da-mi-[ša₂]

He slit the veins of her blood,

r 12'12'

[...] tu₁₅SI.⸢SA₂⸣ a-na bu-⸢su₂⸣-ra-tu₂ ⸢uš⸣-[ta-bil]

he made [...] the north wind carry (it) for news.

r 13'13'

[... AD].⸢AD-šu⸣ iḫ-du-u₂ i-ri-[šu]

His fathers [...], they became joyful, they rejoiced,

r 14'14'

[...] ⸢u₂⸣-ša₂-bi-lu šu-nu a-na ša₂-a-[šu]

they had delivered [...], they to him.

r 15'15'

[i-nu-uḫ₂]-⸢ma⸣ be-lu₄ ša₂-lam-taš i-bar-[ri]

The lord [rested], looking at her corpse,

r 16'16'

[... u₂]-⸢za⸣-ʾa-a-za i-ban-na-a nik-la₂-a-[ti]

intending to divide [...] to create artful things.

r 17'17'

[...] ⸢ma⸣-aš-ṭe-e a-⸢na⸣ ši-ni-[šu]

[...] dried [...] into her two parts,

r 18'18'

[... uṣ]-⸢ṣal⸣-[lil]

[...] he made [...] into a roof.

r 19'19'

[...] ⸢u₂⸣-ša₂-⸢aṣ⸣-[bit]

[...], he posted [...],

(rest of reverse missing)
